name: Middleware / integration / messaging
summary: A system that moves, routes, and transforms messages/events between otherwise-independent systems, decoupling producers from consumers with defined delivery guarantees. Throughput-bound, correctness-critical plumbing where the contract is the message, not a UI.

concerns: delivery-guarantee semantics stated explicitly: at-most-once vs at-least-once vs effectively-exactly-once, and consumer-side idempotency/deduplication when exactly-once is not achievable end-to-end
message durability and persistence: acknowledgement model, no message loss on broker/consumer crash, and replication/quorum for the log/queue
poison-message and failure handling: bounded retries with backoff, dead-letter queue (DLQ), and redrive so one bad message cannot block or be lost
no-loss under failure: producer confirms, consumer offset/ack commit ordering, and recovery/replay after outage
transactional / outbox patterns where a message and a state change must be atomic
questions: Is the delivery guarantee (at-most/at-least/exactly-once) stated, with consumer-side idempotency/dedup where exactly-once isn't end-to-end?
Are durability, acknowledgement, and replication specified so no message is lost on broker/consumer crash?
Are poison-message handling (DLQ, bounded retry, redrive) and recovery/replay after outage defined?

concerns: message schema/contract governance: schema registry, versioning, and enforced backward/forward compatibility so producers and consumers evolve independently
protocol mediation across endpoints: AMQP, MQTT, Kafka protocol, JMS, HTTP/webhooks, gRPC — and translation between them
payload format and transformation: canonical data model, format conversion (JSON/Avro/Protobuf/XML/EDI), and envelope standards (e.g. CloudEvents)
connector/adapter compatibility with source and target system versions
questions: Is message-schema governance (registry, versioning, back/forward compatibility) specified so producers/consumers evolve independently?
Are the protocols to mediate (AMQP/MQTT/Kafka/JMS/HTTP) and their translation defined?
Are payload formats, canonical model, and transformation rules (and envelope standard) specified?

concerns: sustained throughput (messages/sec, MB/sec) and end-to-end latency percentiles from publish to consume
backpressure and flow control: how the system behaves when consumers are slower than producers (buffering, blocking, shedding, rate limiting) without unbounded growth
consumer lag/queue-depth bounds and scaling of partitions/consumers to drain backlog
batching, compression, and prefetch tuning versus latency trade-offs; message-size limits
questions: Are sustained throughput and end-to-end latency percentiles quantified?
Is backpressure behaviour defined when consumers lag producers (no unbounded buffering)?
Are consumer-lag/queue-depth limits and the partition/consumer scaling strategy specified?

concerns: message ordering guarantees and their scope (global vs per-partition/per-key) and how ordering interacts with retries/parallelism
transformation/mapping correctness and data-loss-free format conversion; handling of missing/extra fields
retention/replay window, log compaction, and TTL/expiry of messages
deduplication keys, idempotency tokens, and correlation/trace IDs carried on the envelope
questions: Is the ordering guarantee and its scope (global vs per-key/partition) specified, including interaction with retries?
Are transformation/mapping rules and their correctness (no silent data loss) defined?
Are retention/replay window, TTL, and deduplication/correlation identifiers specified?

concerns: end-to-end traceability: correlation/trace-id propagation and distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) across hops
observability of flow health: throughput, lag, DLQ depth, error rates, and per-route/topic metrics
testability of routes/transformations (contract tests, message replay, sandbox/what-if) without touching live endpoints
message/route auditability and inspection for debugging in-flight data
questions: Is correlation/trace-id propagation and end-to-end tracing across hops required?
Are flow-health metrics (throughput, consumer lag, DLQ depth, error rate) specified?
Are transformations/routes testable (contract tests, replay) and messages auditable?

concerns: authentication/authorization of producers and consumers per topic/queue (who may publish/subscribe to what)
encryption in transit (TLS) and, where required, message-level/payload encryption and PII handling in transit
tenant/domain isolation on shared brokers so one integration cannot read another's messages
message integrity/signing and preventing injection of forged messages into a flow
questions: Are producer/consumer authentication and per-topic/queue authorization specified?
Is transport (and, where needed, payload) encryption required, with PII handling in transit addressed?
Is tenant isolation on shared brokers and message integrity/anti-forgery specified?

concerns: DLQ operations: alerting on DLQ growth, triage, and a defined redrive/reprocess procedure after a fix
runbooks for backlog/lag incidents, broker failover, and consumer-group rebalancing
configuration of routes/topics/connectors and safe change management of live flows
capacity planning for retention storage and partition/consumer sizing
questions: Are DLQ alerting and a redrive/reprocess procedure after fixes defined?
Are runbooks for backlog/lag, broker failover, and rebalancing specified?
Is safe change management of live routes/topics and retention capacity planning addressed?

concerns: routing/filtering correctness: content-based routing, fan-out/fan-in, and splitter/aggregator behaviour (enterprise integration patterns)
completeness of source/target mappings and handling of unmatched/unroutable messages
exception flows: what happens to messages that fail transformation or have no valid route (not just the happy path)
questions: Are routing/filtering rules and aggregation/split behaviour fully specified?
Are all source→target mappings complete, with unmatched/unroutable messages handled?
Is the failure path for messages that cannot be transformed or routed defined?
